summary: High‑energy 1988 Rosemont show with soaring solos and tight jams
blurb: The set rocks from a ripping Jack Straw into a powerful first‑set close with Bird Song → The Promised Land. A seamless second‑set flow features Box of Rain → Iko Iko → Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World, capped by a rousing encore. Jerry’s guitar and Brent’s keys are consistently praised throughout.
review: April 14, 1988 at the Rosemont Horizon delivered one of those marathon Dead marathons that still feels fresh decades later. The first set launches with an explosive Jack Straw solo and rides through West L.A. Fadeaway, Mama Tried and a soaring Bird Song that lands into a triumphant The Promised Land finale. The second half is a masterclass in flow: Box of Rain melts into Iko Iko, then the band erupts on Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World before slipping into a thunder‑filled Drum/Space segment and a fiery The Wheel → Gimme Some Lovin' run that rolls straight into Black Peter, Throwing Stones and a classic Not Fade Away. Brent’s keyboards are “going off” with extra oomph on the Lovin’ groove, while both Mickey and Billy deliver thunderous drumming highlighted by a quirky Caddyshack‑esque voice intro. The show closes on a muddy but heartfelt Black Muddy River encore, leaving the audience buzzing from a 50‑song sprint that’s as tight as it is inspired.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings were analyzed: three positive and one mixed. Reviewers repeatedly praise the band’s tight set flow, standout solos (Jack Straw, Bird Song, Estimated Prophet) and Brent’s keyboard work. The consistency of high‑energy descriptors across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with superb mix, clear vocals and balanced instrument levels that capture the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: High‑energy set flow highlighted by Bird Song → The Promised Land close.
Seamless second‑set transition from Box of Rain into Iko Iko and a fiery Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World jam.
Ripping Jack Straw solo and powerful Big River performance.
Thunderous drumming with a quirky Caddyshackesque voice intro to the drum solo.

band_performance.Jerry: Strong, spirited guitar work and powerful vocals; ripping Big River, explosive Jack Straw solo, soaring solos on West L.A., The Promised Land and Eyes Of The World.
band_performance.Phil: Subtle bass tone on the new instrument, providing nuanced support; especially noticeable during Box of Rain.
band_performance.Bob: Buddy‑Holly style vocal impression on Not Fade Away and tasteful rhythm playing throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards “going off” with great mix, doing what they do best; extra oomph on Gimme Some Lovin' and solid presence across the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Thunderous drumming that drives the first set and adds power to the Drum/Space segment, highlighted by a quirky voice intro.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers the same thunderous energy as Mickey, anchoring the rhythm section with forceful fills and the characteristic Caddyshackesque drum solo intro.
